headline: The Revolving Door Between GLCs and Parliament
context: At least 34 sitting MPs or senators hold directorships in government-linked companies, with combined board fees exceeding RM12 million annually.

cards.big: 34 lawmakers sit on GLC boards. Who are they accountable to?
cards.sub: They vote on policies affecting companies that pay their director fees.

cards.big: No Malaysian law prohibits MPs from holding GLC directorships.
cards.sub: The UK bans it. Singapore restricts it. Malaysia's Code of Ethics is non-binding.

cards.big: GLCs control 36% of Bursa Malaysia market capitalisation.
cards.sub: Policy decisions by lawmakers who sit on these boards move billions.

cards.big: Post-GE15 cabinet: 6 of 28 ministers held GLC roles within 12 months.
cards.sub: The revolving door spins in both directions. No cooling-off period exists.

cards.big: Conflict of interest is not corruption. It is the precondition.
cards.sub: The system does not prevent it because the system is built by the people it would constrain.

cards.big: Publish every directorship. Mandate cooling-off periods. The resistance will tell you everything.
